channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🕊️ Iran and Israel halt military operations after 48-hour exchange of fire
item.description: Both countries called a halt to military operations after the most intense exchange of fire in months. Irans president said the country had not abandoned peace negotiations despite the escalation. | Source: Financial Times

item.title: 🇦🇲🇪🇺 Armenias pro-Europe party wins election, cementing shift away from Russia
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyans Civil Contract party won parliamentary elections, confirming Armenias pivot toward Europe and away from its traditional ally Russia despite Moscows pressure campaign. | Source: AP News

item.title: ⚖️ ICC prosecutor Karim Khan suspended amid sexual misconduct investigation
item.description: The International Criminal Courts chief prosecutor has been suspended while member states consider disciplinary measures, marking a major crisis for the tribunal investigating war crimes in Ukraine and Gaza. | Source: Reuters

item.title: ⛽🇺🇦 Kremlin admits fuel supply problems after Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy sites
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov acknowledged certain problems with fuel supplies in southern Russia after a surge in Ukrainian airstrikes on Russian energy and fuel infrastructure disrupted distribution. | Source: Guardian

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Kim Jong Un hosts Xi Jinping from position of rare strength in Pyongyang summit
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un gave Chinas Xi Jinping a grand welcome at a rare summit in Pyongyang, projecting unity while Xi sought to remind Kim that China remains the senior partner in their relationship. | Source: NBC News

item.title: ⚔️ Israel and Iran Halt Strikes After Trump Warning
item.description: Israel and Iran paused military strikes following a direct appeal from US President Donald Trump, who warned Netanyahu that Israel would be "on your own" if it resumed attacks. The temporary ceasefire comes after months of conflict that has disrupted global oil markets. | Source: Reuters

item.title: ☢️ IAEA Urges Iran to Re-Engage on Nuclear Inspections
item.description: UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi called on Iran to allow inspections at sites bombed by the US and Israel. The US led a push for a resolution at the IAEA board demanding Iran resume cooperation. | Source: Reuters

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Edge Higher Amid Iran-Israel Uncertainty
item.description: Oil prices rose slightly as markets awaited clarity on whether the Iran-Israel halt would hold. The Strait of Hormuz remains a focal point, with Iran's envoy saying transit fees would apply. | Source: Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon Says Israel Bombed It Nearly 3,500 Times During Ceasefire
item.description: Israel conducted nearly 3,500 airstrikes on Lebanon since the April ceasefire announcement, Lebanon's defense minister said. Lebanese President Aoun appealed directly to Israel to pursue negotiations instead of war. | Source: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping Visits North Korea for First Summit in Seven Years
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for his first visit in seven years, meeting with Kim Jong Un. The summit follows Putin's recent visit and signals deepening coordination between China and North Korea. | Source: AP News

item.title: 🇲🇾 Malaysia opposition in turmoil as Islamist party PAS cuts ties with Bersatu
item.description: PAS ended its cooperation with Bersatu, gutting the Perikatan Nasional opposition coalition ahead of key elections in Malaysia. | Source: SCMP

item.title: 🌏 Asian stocks track Wall Street tech bounce as oil eases on Mideast ceasefire hopes
item.description: Asian markets rebounded as investors returned to AI stocks while crude prices eased following Israel-Iran hostilities pause. | Source: France 24

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Names First Female Prime Minister in Decades to Lead AI Push
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung nominated former Naver CEO Han Seong-sook as prime minister, tasking her with driving South Korea's AI-driven economic transformation. | Source: Bloomberg

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Prepares 21st Sanctions Package Against Russia Worth $1.5 Trillion
item.description: The European Union is finalizing its 21st sanctions package against Russia, targeting additional sectors to increase economic pressure on Moscow. | Source: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Blames US for Latest Exchanges of Fire with Israel
item.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry says overnight strikes will worsen an already chaotic diplomatic process with Washington, exchanging messages in an atmosphere of extreme suspicion. | Source: Reuters

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Seeks Calm with Ukraine Over WWII Army Unit Dispute
item.description: Polish PM Tusk calls for solidarity and talks after diplomatic relations deteriorated over Ukraine naming an army unit for nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles in World War Two. | Source: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi and Kim Agree to Expand Cooperation at Pyongyang Summit
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un held a rare summit in Pyongyang, agreeing to expand cooperation in politics, economy and culture. | Source: Reuters

item.title: 📉 Gulf Stock Markets Drop Amid Renewed Middle East Tensions
item.description: Most stock markets in the Gulf ended lower on Monday as renewed conflict in the Middle East weighed on investor sentiment. | Source: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🛢️ US Military Disables Oil Tanker Headed to Iran in Gulf of Oman
item.description: US forces disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman as the unladen vessel headed toward Iran, escalating enforcement of the US naval blockade amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict. | Source: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇮🇷🚢 Iran Threatens to Defeat US Naval Blockade as Tensions Persist
item.description: Iran top negotiator vowed to turn the US naval blockade into another defeat for the enemy, as Tehran faces increased maritime interdiction of its oil exports during the ongoing conflict with Israel. | Source: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇺🇸📜 US Judge Rules Trump H-1B Visa Fee Unlawful
item.description: A federal judge declared the $100,000 fee imposed by President Trump on new H-1B visas for highly skilled workers unlawful, dealing a blow to the administration immigration restrictions. | Source: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine recaptures over 600 square kilometers of territory in 2026
item.description: Ukraine's military chief reports retaking more than 600 sq km this year, with independent battlefield mappers confirming Russia's advances have slowed or reversed in recent months. | Source: Reuters

item.title: ⛽ Russia's fuel crisis deepens as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories
item.description: Ukrainian attacks on Russian supply lines have made it difficult for Moscow to provide military and civilian supplies to occupied territories, intensifying pressure on Russian logistics. | Source: BBC

item.title: ⚖️ ICC Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan Suspended Over Sexual Misconduct Allegations
item.description: The International Criminal Court's oversight body suspended Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan late Monday, referring him for disciplinary proceedings. Khan faces allegations of sexual misconduct with a female aide spanning more than two years, which he denies. | Source: AP News

item.title: ☢️ Nuclear Weapons Spending Hits Record $119 Billion as Arms Race Intensifies
item.description: Global spending on nuclear weapons rose 19% in 2025 to $119 billion, the highest since tracking began. The US led with $69.2 billion, more than all other nuclear states combined, while China increased spending by 7% to $13.5 billion. | Source: Reuters
